paper.title_en: Optical observations on the young Type Ia SN 2021fxy with detached high velocity features
paper.abstract_en: We present optical observations on the young type Ia supernova (SN Ia) SN 2021fxy obtained within a few days after the explosion, with a focus on its prominent high-velocity features (HVFs). It reached a $B$-band maximum of $M_{\rm max}(B) = -19.36\pm0.31$ mag, corresponding to a bolometric luminosity of $\sim 1.3\times10^{43}~\rm{erg~s^{-1}}$ with a synthesized $^{56}$Ni mass of $0.58\pm0.14$ M$_{\odot}$. The early spectra exhibit strong HVFs of intermediate-mass elements that are significantly detached from the photospheric components. In particular, the velocity of the Si II $\lambda6355$ HVFs follows a power-law evolution ($β\approx 0.1$), shallower than the expected photospheric velocity evolution expected for an assumed $n=10$ density profile ($β\approx 0.22$) under homologous expansion. This behavior is consistent with the HVFs forming in intrinsic ejecta structures at least partially decoupled from the bulk outer ejecta, providing a possible constraint on the explosion physics of SNe Ia.
